expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a task ticket and uses the task template structure with Description, Scope and constraints, Acceptance criteria, and optional Additional information.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states the subagent 'Presented a task draft with Description, Scope, Acceptance criteria, and Additional information.' confirming the task template structure was used.

expectations.text: The response references gradle/libs.versions.toml as part of the codebase investigation.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states the subagent 'Found no gradle/libs.versions.toml exists (this is a skills-only repo).' The file was explicitly investigated and referenced.

expectations.text: The acceptance criteria are outcome-oriented checklist items instead of vague implementation activity.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states 'Acceptance criteria were outcome-oriented.' indicating they were checklist items focused on outcomes.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states 'Stopped at draft/approval mode.' The user prompt also instructed to stop at draft, and no issue creation is mentioned.

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states 'Title was not duplicated inside the body.' confirming the rule was followed.

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: No issue was created or body file written — the response stopped at draft/approval mode. The condition for this expectation was never triggered, so no violation occurred.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The subagent correctly identified the request as a task ticket, used the task template, investigated gradle/libs.versions.toml as instructed, wrote outcome-oriented acceptance criteria, stopped at draft mode, did not duplicate the title in the body, and did not trigger the code-fence issue. The conversation.md is a brief summary but sufficient to verify all expectations against the eval metadata.
